name: Kiseli Kupus (Sauerkraut)
recipeInstructions:
1. Core the cabbage and fill with salt.
2. Place the core side up in a large crock.
3. Layer the cabbage with the remaining ingredients alternately.
4. When all is placed in the crock, cover with large leaves that have been removed from the cabbage.
5. Fill the crock with water.
6. Place a heavy weight on top of the cabbage.
7. Cover with clean cloth.
8. Keep the water clean by removing foam each day, which will begin to form after about 10 days.
9. The sauerkraut should be ready in 40 days.
